old_claim.note: Gate-All-Around Nanosheet Transistors is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: Intel describes PowerVia and RibbonFET/GAA as co-introduced scaling technologies while also stating PowerVia was developed separately from transistor development.

source_shape.source_locator: Intel Newsroom discussion of PowerVia testing, 20A/18A integration, and decoupling PowerVia development from RibbonFET transistor development.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Intel reports PowerVia backside power delivery for 20A/18A and says PowerVia was tested separately before integration with RibbonFET gate-all-around technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a commercial scaling relationship because the source ties both technologies to the same advanced-node roadmap while explicitly rejecting a direct transistor-development dependency.

ontology_before: The graph made backside power delivery require GAA nanosheet transistors, turning a co-adopted node-scaling technology into a hard transistor-architecture dependency.
ontology_after: The graph keeps the roadmap association between backside power and GAA/RibbonFET while grounding backside power delivery in semiconductor process nodes and wafer/process integration.

demotion_preserves.reason: Backside power delivery remains tied to advanced semiconductor process-node integration rather than floating as an unrelated technique.
Wafer thinning, polishing, and backside process integration remain represented through the CMP process-enabler edge.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: backside power delivery stays connected to GAA/RibbonFET as a co-scaling roadmap technology. Changed: the edge no longer claims GAA is a hard prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A source showed backside power delivery cannot be built, tested, or defined without GAA nanosheet transistors.
The graph removed process-node and backside-processing enablers while weakening the GAA edge.
A future split created an Intel PowerVia-specific node that should carry a stronger relation to RibbonFET than the generic backside-power node.
short_reason: Backside power delivery and GAA/RibbonFET are co-scaling roadmap technologies, but Intel describes PowerVia development as decoupled from transistor development.
